beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check hives and garden beds in backyard
beats.reasoning: Early riser who prioritizes checking bee colonies and garden first thing on a sunny morning; part of daily routine as a beekeeper.

beats.activity: Bike to U-District farmers market to set up bee products and local honey stand
beats.reasoning: Monday morning is prime farmers market setup; River bikes across town to manage the weekly honey and bee-product sales booth.

beats.activity: Tend farmers market stall, sell honey and bee products, chat with regulars
beats.reasoning: Core work activity; River runs the market stall and enjoys the regulars' company; timing ends before the FIFA match to allow for a break.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby bar to catch USA vs Wales FIFA match kickoff
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is down 9am–3pm, but River can easily walk from farmers market to a bar in U-District for the 11am match; skeptical but enjoys the vibe.

beats.activity: Watch FIFA match at the bar, grab lunch and a beer, chat with other fans
beats.reasoning: River is skeptical but not opposed to a morning match with a crowd; takes a leisurely lunch break during the game, avoiding the farmers market rush.

beats.activity: Walk back to farmers market to close down stall
beats.reasoning: Short walk back to pack up and secure the market booth after the match; Light Rail closure doesn't affect this walkable errand.

beats.activity: Pack up market stall, load honey and products onto bike, inventory check
beats.reasoning: Closing down the stall is part of River's work; takes time to secure goods and check for the week ahead.

beats.activity: Bike to Ballard to thrift at local vintage shops
beats.reasoning: River loves thrifting and takes advantage of the sunny afternoon to bike across town; avoids Light Rail closure by using bike and side streets.

beats.activity: Browse vintage shops and thrift stores, hunt for transit maps and hidden treasures
beats.reasoning: River's favorite leisure activity; hunts for transit maps from different cities to add to collection; perfect weather for browsing and wandering.

beats.activity: Bike home via Wallingford, stopping at QFC for evening groceries
beats.reasoning: Natural route home; River picks up fresh produce and supplies for the evening; combines errand with commute.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, tend to hives and garden as evening light fades
beats.reasoning: River cooks a simple dinner and uses the golden-hour light to do evening garden maintenance and hive checks before winding down.

beats.activity: Board game night with close friends, light snacks and conversation
beats.reasoning: River values time with close friends; hosts a small board game night at home on a beautiful Monday evening; intimate gathering over big crowds.

diary: Monday morning started like most do—coffee in hand, checking on the hives before the heat set in. The bees were calm, the garden beds looking lush. I biked to the U-District market and settled into the rhythm of selling honey to familiar faces. Around eleven, the FIFA buzz hit the neighborhood, so I locked up early and wandered over to Monkey Loft. The place was already packed. I caught the USA match with a cold beer and some genuinely good nachos, cheering with strangers who suddenly felt like neighbors. The energy was infectious. After packing up the stall, I had time to kill before dinner, so I pedaled over to Ballard and lost an hour in Buffalo Exchange hunting for old transit maps—found a beautiful 1987 King County one. Home felt good tonight. Made a simple pasta, watered the garden as the light went gold, and then friends came by for board games. We played until late, the kind of night where time doesn't matter.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure actually worked in my favor—no stress about timing, just a straightforward bike commute. I was torn about leaving the market early for the match, but the sunny weather and communal energy pulling people downtown felt too good to resist. At fifty-eight, I'm learning to say yes to those moments. The vintage shopping was pure indulgence; I didn't need anything, but Ballard's been calling me lately. Board game night anchored the day nicely—I almost skipped it, thinking I was tired, but friends matter more than fatigue. The whole day had this generous, unhurried quality. The weather helped. Sometimes a good day isn't about accomplishment; it's about showing up and being present.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
